questions.question: What is the difference between user settings and workspace settings in VS Code?
options: User settings apply to every project, workspace settings apply only to one repo
Workspace settings are faster than user settings
User settings only affect terminal commands
Workspace settings only work for JavaScript files
questions.explanation: Workspace settings are scoped to one project, while user settings apply globally to your editor.

questions.question: What does the command palette help you do?
options: Discover and run editor commands quickly
questions.explanation: The command palette is one of the fastest ways to access editor features without hunting through menus.

questions.question: Why are multi-root workspaces useful?
options: They let you edit multiple related folders in one window
questions.explanation: Multi-root workspaces are helpful when you are working across related frontend apps, packages, or docs at the same time.

questions.question: What should a good frontend VS Code setup usually include?
options: A random set of extensions from every category
Formatting, linting, and language-tooling support tuned to the project
A different theme on every file
questions.explanation: The editor should reinforce the project rules for formatting, linting, and language support.
